title: Raw-categorical CatBoost specialist instead of manual frequency encoding
summary: Your strongest runs still hand-engineer category frequencies.
rationale: Your strongest runs still hand-engineer category frequencies. CatBoost explicitly recommends using native categorical handling and categorical combinations rather than preprocessing one-hot/frequency substitutes; this is especially relevant with sparse Driver and Race x Compound interactions.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost model with raw categorical columns and a few high-signal crosses kept as strings, using fold-local fitting only. Keep numeric physics features, but remove most manual freq encodings in this branch so ordered CTRs/combinations do the work. Blend this specialist with the current numeric heuristic model rather than replacing it outright.
expected_effect: Can recover signal from rare drivers/race-compound interactions and reduce brittleness from manually chosen frequency encodings.
risk: If CV is weak, CatBoost can look better locally than on LB; tuning can also overfit sparse identities.
